title: Xeriscape Roasted Mexican tlayudas Wooden-Board Tlayudas
description: A roasted, flaky, brunch-mood Mexican dish featuring Sichuan pepper and an ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly deglaze.

ingredients.name: tlayuda-style toppings
ingredients.notes: such as shredded chicken, avocado, queso fresco, cilantro, lime juice

instructions: Grill wood-fired corn tortillas over medium heat for 2-3 minutes per side, until slightly charred.
Mix Sichuan peppercorns, chipotle peppers, garlic, onion, cumin, salt, black pepper, and ayurvedic rajasic spice blend in a small bowl.
Brush the tortillas with olive oil and place them on a wooden board or a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast the tortillas in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, until crispy and slightly puffed.
Remove the tortillas from the oven and brush with apple cider vinegar for deglaze.
Allow the tortillas to cool for 2-3 minutes before topping with tlayuda-style toppings.
Cut the tlayudas into triangles or squares and serve immediately.
Garnish with wood smoke powder for added flavor and aroma.
Serve with a side of salsa, avocado, or sour cream, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
